questions.question: Explain the concept of an angle with suitable examples.
questions.answer: An angle is formed when two rays meet at a common point called the vertex. The two rays are called arms of the angle. Examples include 30°, 90°, and 120°.

questions.question: Explain the different types of angles with examples.
questions.answer: Angles are classified based on their measures. Acute angles are less than 90°, right angles are equal to 90°, obtuse angles are between 90° and 180°, straight angles are 180°, and reflex angles are greater than 180° but less than 360°.

questions.question: Explain complementary and supplementary angles with examples.
questions.answer: Complementary angles are two angles whose sum is 90°. Example: 30° and 60°. Supplementary angles are two angles whose sum is 180°. Example: 110° and 70°.

questions.question: Explain adjacent angles with a diagram description.
questions.answer: Adjacent angles are two angles that share a common vertex and a common arm but do not overlap. For example, angles ∠ABC and ∠CBD share the common vertex B and arm BC.

questions.question: Explain the concept of a linear pair of angles.
questions.answer: A linear pair consists of two adjacent angles whose non-common arms form a straight line. The sum of the angles in a linear pair is always 180°.

questions.question: State and explain the linear pair axiom.
questions.answer: The linear pair axiom states that if a ray stands on a line, then the sum of the two adjacent angles formed is 180°.

questions.question: Explain vertically opposite angles with an example.
questions.answer: When two lines intersect, the angles opposite each other are called vertically opposite angles. These angles are always equal.

questions.question: State the theorem of vertically opposite angles.
questions.answer: When two lines intersect each other, vertically opposite angles are equal.

questions.question: Explain the concept of parallel lines.
questions.answer: Parallel lines are lines in the same plane that never intersect and remain at a constant distance from each other.

questions.question: Explain the concept of a transversal.
questions.answer: A transversal is a line that intersects two or more lines at different points.

questions.question: Explain corresponding angles with an example.
questions.answer: Corresponding angles are angles that occupy the same relative position when a transversal cuts two lines. When the lines are parallel, corresponding angles are equal.

questions.question: Explain alternate interior angles with an example.
questions.answer: Alternate interior angles are angles formed inside two lines on opposite sides of a transversal. If the lines are parallel, these angles are equal.

questions.question: Explain interior angles on the same side of a transversal.
questions.answer: Interior angles on the same side of a transversal are angles between two lines and on the same side of the transversal. Their sum is 180° when the lines are parallel.

questions.question: Explain the sum of angles around a point.
questions.answer: The sum of all angles around a point is always 360°.

questions.question: Explain the relation between linear pair and supplementary angles.
questions.answer: Angles forming a linear pair always sum to 180°, therefore they are supplementary.

questions.question: Explain why vertically opposite angles are equal.
questions.answer: When two lines intersect, adjacent angles form a linear pair. Using this property, it can be proved that vertically opposite angles are equal.

questions.question: Explain the properties of parallel lines.
questions.answer: Parallel lines never intersect and remain at a constant distance. When cut by a transversal, they form equal corresponding and alternate interior angles.

questions.question: Explain reflex angles with examples.
questions.answer: A reflex angle is an angle greater than 180° but less than 360°. Example: 210°.

questions.question: Explain straight angles with examples.
questions.answer: A straight angle measures exactly 180° and forms a straight line.

questions.question: Explain right angles with examples.
questions.answer: A right angle measures exactly 90°. Example: the corner of a square.

questions.question: Explain acute angles with examples.
questions.answer: An acute angle is an angle measuring less than 90°. Example: 45°.

questions.question: Explain obtuse angles with examples.
questions.answer: An obtuse angle measures more than 90° but less than 180°. Example: 120°.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between complementary and right angles.
questions.answer: Two complementary angles together form a right angle because their sum is 90°.

questions.question: Explain the relation between supplementary angles and straight angles.
questions.answer: Two supplementary angles together form a straight angle because their sum is 180°.

questions.question: Explain the importance of angles in geometry.
questions.answer: Angles help measure rotation and direction in geometry. They are used in studying shapes, triangles, polygons, and many geometric constructions.
